mission.title: jaeger: Push official images to quay.io, in addition to Docker Hub
mission.description: Push official images to quay.io, in addition to Docker Hub. Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current jaeger deployment
steps.description:
Verify your jaeger version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger
helm list -n observability 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working jaeger installation.

steps.title: Review jaeger configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant jaeger configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger
kubectl get configmap -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=jaeger
```
# Requirement - what kind of business use case are you trying to solve?

High availability and resiliency of Jaeger in production environments

## Problem - what in Jaeger blocks you from solving the requirement?

Currently, the official images for

steps.title: Apply the fix for Push official images to quay.io, in addition to Docker Hub
steps.description:
## Which problem is this PR solving?
- pushes official images to quay.io in addition to docker.io

## Short description of the changes
- addition of upload-to-quay script which pushes docker images to the quay.io platform
```yaml
+ docker push quay.io/jaegertracing/example-hotrod
Using default tag: latest
The push refers to repository [quay.io/jaegertracing/example-hotrod]
a93b60b49a79: Preparing
unauthorized: access to the requested resource is not authorized
Error: Process completed with exit code 1.
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger
kubectl get events -n observability --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Push official images to quay.io, in addition to Docker Hub" is functioning correctly.
